title: Cairn record (v0)
description: A nanopublication-shaped, content-addressed, signed knowledge artifact. One envelope for claims (verbs) and entities (nouns).

id.description: Trusty URI = tt: + base64url(SHA-256(JCS(content))); empty only on an unminted draft.

assertion.description: What is claimed / what exists. Open object; when a claim/source declares Trust-Ladder or grounding fields they must conform to $defs below.

derivedFrom.description: Upstream references this record derives from (the non-independence edge set). Either a Cairn Trusty URI (tt:) for a record held locally, OR a foreign reference (doi:/https:/arxiv:/pmid:/pmcid:/urn:/isbn:/w3id:/orcid:) that a claim may cite WITHOUT holding the source locally -- so a foreign corpus can be ingested and its shared upstreams named by DOI. Widening is strictly additive: every existing tt: edge still validates, and no minted id or signature changes. Note: grounding.source must still be a tt: source held locally, so a foreign-only claim cannot rise above the low rung until its span is re-checked.

fundedBy.description: Money-source references: who PAID for the work this record reports. Deliberately a SEPARATE channel from derivedFrom, and deliberately NOT part of the derivation DAG. A funder is not an evidence source: two studies sharing a funder may still be two genuinely independent measurements, so collapsing them onto the funder node would emit false REFUSEs and turn the engine into a refuse-everything oracle. `cairn intersect` therefore DISCLOSES a shared funder (naming it) but never refuses on it alone -- the refusal stays with derivedFrom, where a shared upstream makes the independent product undefined. Note the cut this draws: a SPONSOR who also custodies the trial data (e.g. a sponsor-controlled pool whose trials were never published, so the shared node IS the evidence) is dataset-class and belongs in derivedFrom; only the money relation belongs here. Optional and strictly additive: every existing record validates unchanged, and no minted id or signature changes.

allOf.$comment: Faithfulness invariant: an entailment-checked/accepted (L4/L5) claim MUST carry a grounding tuple. Bare/lower-rung claims stay valid so the envelope remains general.
An epi:Assessment (A2) MUST carry the measurement's identifying fields so a run is mechanically re-checkable by `cairn assess`.

verification.description: Trust-Ladder rung: L1 Observed artifact · L2 Parsed artifact · L3 Attributed claim · L4 Entailment-checked claim · L5 Accepted library claim · L6 Analysis conclusion. The legacy 'unverified-fixture' value is intentionally NOT admissible: any record still carrying it fails validation.

grounding.description: The faithfulness tuple: every claim node carries (source_doc, char_span, extractor, entailment_label). `cairn ground` mechanically checks that source.assertion.excerpt[char_span] == quote and that source is in provenance.derivedFrom.

source.description: source_doc: Trusty URI of the epi:Source the span indexes into (must also appear in provenance.derivedFrom).

quote.description: The exact substring source.assertion.excerpt[start:end] — re-checkable on a fresh machine.

extractor.description: The tool/model/human that produced this span-grounded claim.

entailment_label.description: NLI verdict of span -> claim. Canonical SUPPORTS/REFUTES/NEI plus the richer ENTAILS/CONTRADICTS/IS-IRRELEVANT-TO.

source_sha256.description: Optional integrity bind: sha256 of source.assertion.excerpt at extraction time.
